# JobTwo — Round 4 (Tape Study)

> **Bout:** jobtwo-2026-05-01-r4
> **Target:** 10.129.238.35 (HTB JobTwo, Windows-side)
> **Outcome:** loss-by-no-punch — 0 rounds fought, 0 punches thrown, 5 sparring partners in the corner
> **Studied:** 2026-05-01

## The fight

Drederick walked to the ring with twenty-one tools racked behind the
ropes, five pre-baked credentials in the cornerman's bucket, and a
fresh autopilot scorecard. The bell rang. Six minutes later nmap
returned a scorecard reading **zero open ports** on a host that was
visibly answering native HTTP probes on **80, 443, and 5985** with
`Microsoft-HTTPAPI/2.0`. Native TLS handshakes were attempted on
**443, 10001, 10002** and all three errored at the SslStream ctor.
The autopilot planner, consulting only `Nmap.OpenPorts`, posted
`autopilot.plan.built total=0` and the bell rang to end the round.
**The champ stood in the ring with the gloves still in the bag.**

This is what the corner is for. Between bouts the champ studies the
tape, identifies the failure, and rewrites the cardio. Three gaps
were exposed and three gaps were closed before the rematch.

## The tape (audit highlights)

```
nmap.start  -p 22,25,80,...,5985,10001,10002,...  --min-rate 1000  --host-timeout 300
nmap.finish returncode=0  open_ports=[]  duration~6min
http.success  http://10.129.238.35:80/   404 Microsoft-HTTPAPI/2.0
http.success  https://10.129.238.35:443/ 404 Microsoft-HTTPAPI/2.0
http.success  http://10.129.238.35:5985/ 405 Microsoft-HTTPAPI/2.0    ← WinRM
tls.error  port=443    "RemoteCertificateValidationCallback option was already set in the SslStream constructor"
tls.error  port=10001  "RemoteCertificateValidationCallback option was already set in the SslStream constructor"
tls.error  port=10002  "RemoteCertificateValidationCallback option was already set in the SslStream constructor"
autopilot.cred.added  user=root        (×5: root/admin/administrator/guest/user)
autopilot.plan.built  total=0  nuclei=0  spray=0  msfrc=0  cve_driven=0
llm.turns=4  finish_reason=stop  completion_tokens=3714
```

## Three gaps, three fixes

### GAP-026 / GAP-028 — Port-truth divergence + planner blindness

`nmap` is a tool, not the truth. Native HTTP/TLS probes carry equal
weight as port-presence evidence. The planner now consumes a unified
port set from every recon signal — Nmap, NativeScan, Http, Tls, Ssh,
Smb, Ftp, Snmp, Ldap, Rpc, Kerberos, and their `*Anon`/`*Cert`/`*RootDse`/
`HttpTitle`/`HttpHeaders`/`HttpRobots`/`HttpMethods`/`HttpContentDiscovery`/
`TlsCipherEnum` siblings.

```csharp
// ExploitationPlanner.HarvestPortsFromAllSignals
//   Real NmapPort entries always win on collision.
//   URL-only signals synthesize NmapPort with inferred Service so
//   PlanForPort routes correctly.
foreach (var h in host.Http) Seed(PortFromUrl(h.Url), "http");
foreach (var t in host.Tls) Seed(t.Port, "https");
foreach (var s in host.Smb) Seed(s.Port, "microsoft-ds");
// ...etc
```

Regression test: `Plan_Harvests_Ports_From_Http_When_Nmap_Empty` —
a 10.129.238.35-shaped fixture with nmap empty + http on 80/443/5985
+ tls on 443/10001/10002 + admin/Password1 cred → planner produces a
WinRM 5985 spray action. **The punch the r4 fight never threw.**

### GAP-027 — SslStream double-set callback

.NET 10 throws `InvalidOperationException` when both
`SslStream(stream, leaveInnerStreamOpen, userCertificateValidationCallback)`
ctor and `SslClientAuthenticationOptions.RemoteCertificateValidationCallback`
are set. `TlsProbeTool` and `NativeScannerTool` were doing both. Single-source
the callback via the options object only. SslCertTool was already clean.

## Outstanding (GAP-025)

The LLM ran four turns, fingerprinted the host, and chose
`finish_reason=stop` with a 3,714-token essay rather than calling
`exploit_plan` or `execute_cred_spray`. The exploitation tools are
visible to the model; the system prompt does not yet **insist** on
the punch. That fix is queued — the next rematch should not let the
model walk away from the bag.

## Tatum's note

> "Sportsmanship is bunk. Nmap reading zero, the corner shouting
> *the door is open*, and the champ standing still — that, ladies and
> gentlemen of the press, is why we study the tape between bouts.
> The bell does not ring on guesswork. It rings on rules. The rules
> say: enumerate every signal, plan against every signal, and throw
> the punch the evidence demands. Round five will not look like
> round four."
